title: find_matches truncates with .head(limit) BEFORE sorting by date
evidence: brazilian_soccer_mcp/query_handlers.py:74-75 — `df = df.head(limit)` runs before `df = df.sort_values('date', ascending=False)`; all_matches() concatenates 6 CSVs so pre-sort row order is not chronological, so when a filtered query yields more than `limit` rows the tool returns an arbitrary subset (roughly the earliest datasets/rows) and only sorts within it — not the most-recent N matches the description implies.
suggestion: Sort by date first, then apply .head(limit): `df = df.sort_values('date', ascending=False).head(limit)`.

title: Team filtering uses unanchored substring contains, causing over-broad matches
evidence: brazilian_soccer_mcp/query_handlers.py:41-42 (find_matches), 110-111 (get_team_statistics), 185-188 (get_head_to_head) — `str.contains(team_norm)` matches any team whose name contains the query, e.g. team='America' also matches 'Americano', 'America-MG', 'America-RN', inflating stats/standings for common substrings.
suggestion: Match on normalized equality (or word-boundary) after normalize_team_name rather than raw substring contains.
